images.alt:
Mit einer Illustration wurde bekannt, dass der romantische Fantasy Komödie Manga »Dark Summoner to Dekiteiru« von Shaoh eine Short Anime-Adaption erhält. Der Anime soll im Laufe des Jahres 2026 im japanischen TV starten. Weitere Informationen zum Produktionsteam und Sprechercast sind bislang nicht bekannt.

Die Reihe erscheint seit Dezember 2021 im Online Magazine Dra Dra Sharp# von Kadokawa und umfasst bislang 7 Bände. Der Manga ist bislang weder auf Englisch noch auf Deutsch erhältlich.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Als die Halbmenschin und dunkle Beschwörerin Amona der Gruppe des rechtschaffenen Klerikers Roni beitritt, rechnen alle mit einem sofortigen Zusammenstoß. Schließlich nutzt sie die Macht von Dämonen, während er für Reinheit und Glauben steht. Doch zwischen Misstrauen, Gegensätzen und gefährlicher Magie entsteht schneller eine Anziehung, als irgendjemand erwartet hätte.

record.text:
Golden Reflections

I saw this greater yellowlegs in Albany, Georgia about a years ago.

images.alt: “In this captivating photograph titled “Golden Reflections,” a greater yellowlegs stands gracefully on the clay bank of a serene pond in Albany, GA. The bird’s feathers are a delicate shade of light gray, adorned with intricate speckles in black and brown. Its long legs, a vibrant yellow, anchor it firmly in the water, where its feet rest ankle-deep. As the yellowlegs gazes into the mirror-like pond, its shadow stretches across the sun-kissed clay bank. Its beak, slender and pointed, hints at its foraging prowess, while its dark eye reflects both curiosity and the wisdom of a waterbird attuned to its surroundings. ." - Microsoft Copilot with edits

TL;DR I tested GitHub Copilot CLI running offline with local models on a CPU-only Microsoft Dev Box. The short version: Ollama worked for basic offline prompts, but struggled with long-context agentic workflows and tool…

external.description: TL;DR I tested GitHub Copilot CLI running offline with local models on a CPU-only Microsoft Dev Box. The short version: Ollama worked for basic offline prompts, but struggled with long-context agentic workflows and tool execution on CPU-only hardware. LM Studio worked better as the local OpenAI-compatible provider. google/gemma-4-e2b successfully executed Copilot CLI PowerShell tools. 32K and 64K context were the practical sweet spot for guided development.

record.text: You also get the chance to learn something in the process! If you're on slightly unfamiliar turf, a "what sort of analysis would be appropriate here" question up front can give you something to go away and learn about and save you from (e.g.) trying to take conclusions from underpowered data

Claude (for instance) has all the tools to do that; less sure that MS Copilot on default settings does, I've not checked. But that sequence keeps the action keeps what the AI's doing within the realm of what it's good at, and it gives you something repeatable when you find your data needed fixes

record.text: VECT 2.0 Ransomware Bug Turns Malware Into a Permanent Data Wiper #CheckPointresearch #datawipermalware #malware

external.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a major flaw in the VECT 2.0 ransomware that causes the malware to permanently destroy large files instead of properly encrypting them, making recovery impossible even if victims decide to pay a ransom.
The ransomware operation has reportedly been promoted on newer versions of BreachForums, where the group invited users to join its affiliate program. Interested participants were allegedly given access keys through private messages.
VECT operators also announced a collaboration with TeamPCP, the threat actor linked to recent supply-chain attacks targeting Trivy, LiteLLM, Telnyx, and even the European Commission. According to the announcement, the partnership aimed to exploit victims affected by those supply-chain breaches by deploying ransomware payloads and expanding attacks against additional organizations.
Critical Encryption Flaw Discovered
Researchers found that VECT 2.0 contains a serious issue in how it manages encryption nonces during the file-encryption process. Although the ransomware was designed to speed up encryption for large files, the implementation accidentally overwrites nonce data during each encryption cycle.
Because the malware uses the same memory buffer repeatedly for nonce generation, every newly created nonce replaces the previous one. Once the encryption process is completed, only the final nonce remains stored and is written to disk.
This mistake means that only the last 25% of an affected file can potentially be recovered, while the remaining portions become permanently inaccessible due to the missing nonces.
The problem becomes even more severe because the lost nonces are not sent back to the attackers either. As a result, even the ransomware operators themselves would be unable to decrypt victim files after payment.
Security researchers warned that the flaw effectively transforms the ransomware into a destructive data wiper, particularly in enterprise environments where most valuable assets exceed the malware’s file-size threshold.
“At a threshold of only 128 KB, smaller than a typical email attachment or office document, what the code classifies as a large file encompasses not just VM disks, databases, and backups, but routine documents, spreadsheets, and mailboxes. In practice, almost nothing a victim would care to recover falls below this boundary,” Check Point says.
Researchers also confirmed that the same nonce-management vulnerability exists across all VECT 2.0 variants, including Windows, Linux, and ESXi versions, meaning the irreversible file destruction behavior impacts every platform supported by the ransomware.
external.title: VECT 2.0 Ransomware Bug Turns Malware Into a Permanent Data Wiper

record.text: Anthropic Probes Alleged Unauthorized Access to Powerful Claude Mythos AI Cybersecurity Model #AIcybersecurity #AIvulnerabilities #Anthropic

external.description:
Anthropic is examining claims that a limited number of individuals may have gained unauthorized access to its highly advanced Claude Mythos AI model, a cybersecurity-focused system the company considers too sensitive for public release.
"We're investigating a report claiming unauthorized access to Claude Mythos Preview through one of our third-party vendor environments," the company said in a statement.
The investigation follows a Bloomberg report alleging that users on a private online forum were able to interact with the model without receiving official authorization.
The Claude Mythos model has attracted significant attention due to its reported ability to identify and exploit security vulnerabilities at scale. While concerns continue to grow around the risks associated with powerful AI systems, some officials believe such tools could ultimately improve cybersecurity if managed responsibly.
Anthropic clarified that there is currently no evidence suggesting its own systems were compromised or that malicious actors have taken control of the model. However, the incident has renewed concerns about whether major AI firms can effectively safeguard advanced frontier AI technologies from unauthorized access.
Cybersecurity experts suggest the issue may not have resulted from a traditional hacking attack. According to Raluca Saceanu, chief executive of cybersecurity firm Smarttech247, the incident was "most likely through misuse of access rather than a classic hack."
Anthropic has reportedly provided select technology and financial organizations with access to the Mythos model to help strengthen their cybersecurity defenses. However, such partnerships rely heavily on third-party organizations maintaining strict internal access controls.
According to Bloomberg, the individual linked to the access claim may have already possessed permission to view Anthropic’s AI systems through work connected to a third-party contractor. The report further stated that the group continued using the model after obtaining access, although they allegedly avoided using it for offensive hacking activities to remain undetected.
"When powerful AI tools are accessed or used outside their intended controls, the risk is not just a security incident but the spread of capabilities that could be used for fraud, cyber abuse, or other malicious activity," Saceanu said.
Meanwhile, UK cybersecurity officials continue to stress both the risks and opportunities presented by advanced AI systems. Speaking at the CyberUK conference, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) chief Richard Horne highlighted how frontier AI technologies are rapidly changing the cybersecurity landscape.
"As we have seen in the media in recent days, frontier AI is rapidly enabling discovery and exploitation of existing vulnerabilities at scale, illustrating how quickly it will expose where fundamentals of cyber-security are still to be addressed," he said.
Horne encouraged organizations not to panic over emerging AI-driven threats but instead focus on strengthening basic cybersecurity practices such as software updates and modernizing outdated IT systems.
During the same event, UK Security Minister Dan Jarvis urged closer collaboration between governments and AI developers to ensure advanced AI technologies are used to protect critical infrastructure and national networks.
Most frontier AI systems are currently being developed by companies based in the United States and China, leaving countries like the UK dependent on foreign firms for access to cutting-edge cybersecurity tools such as Mythos.
The growing role of AI in cybersecurity comes amid rising concerns over cyber warfare and digital attacks linked to nation-state actors, particularly Russia and China. The NCSC has increasingly described cyberspace as the “home front” of modern defense, emphasizing the expanding role of cyber operations in global conflicts.

record.text: Canvas Cyberattack Disrupts Universities Nationwide, Thousands of Schools Potentially Impacted #Canvascyberattack #CyberAttacks #InstructureCanvasbreach

external.description:
A major cybersecurity breach has disrupted online learning systems at universities across the United States, including the University of Minnesota and University of Wisconsin, after hackers reportedly targeted Canvas, a widely used learning management platform owned by Instructure.The outage began Thursday evening, leaving students and faculty unable to access Canvas for coursework, assignments, grades, and communication tools. Online screenshots circulating on social media appear to show a message from the hacking group ShinyHunters claiming responsibility for the attack. The message allegedly advised affected institutions to “consult with a cyber advisory firm and contact us privately… to negotiate a settlement.”
A spokesperson for the University of Minnesota confirmed the incident in an official statement:
“The University of Minnesota was notified by Instructure, a software and technology supplier of the University, of a cybersecurity incident affecting its clients worldwide. As of today, users are unable to access Instructure’s Canvas system, which is a cloud- and web-based learning management system for online courses, learning materials and communications. University administrators are awaiting updates from the vendor and taking additional measures to protect University information.”
The University of Wisconsin also acknowledged being impacted by the widespread outage.
“At around 3 p.m. today, UW–Madison became aware we are part of a nationwide Canvas outage. We recognize this is occurring at a very challenging time during final exams and grading, and we’re committed to providing you with support and flexibility as we navigate this significant disruption. Multiple teams are working to address this issue.”
University officials further warned students not to respond to any suspicious prompts from Canvas, including requests to log in, click links, or reset passwords during the outage period.
Cybersecurity experts say attacks like this are becoming increasingly common because a single breach can affect thousands of institutions simultaneously. Adam Marre, chief information security officer at Arctic Wolf, explained:
“Rather than target one institution, one victim, they can get many at once. So in this case, this Canvas software is one that’s used by thousands of educational institutions across the country and therefore it’s a way for these attackers to get highly leveraged on the victim to get them to pay money, so there’s lots of different victims and they can get lots of information with one attack.”
Marre also cautioned users to remain alert against phishing and social engineering attempts following the breach.
“They really need to watch out especially for social engineering attacks. These are the types of attacks that come as emails, texts, direct messages that look innocuous, but they’re really someone trying to trick you, defraud you, do something to further this crime, and so what they want to do is create a sense of urgency to get you to not think, not pause and just act quickly.”
He advised users to avoid clicking suspicious links, directly access platforms through official websites, and ensure multifactor authentication remains enabled on all accounts.
“When attackers get this kind of information or the kind of information that may be involved in this attack, things like emails, names, maybe direct messages, it’s good to remember attackers don’t always use this right away. Often they pause and wait sometimes even months before then using this in phishing attacks and other social engineering attacks.”Marre added:
“We always need to be on guard when we’re online.”
Canvas is a widely adopted digital education platform used for assignments, lecture videos, grading systems, and academic communication. According to Luke Connolly, a threat analyst at Emisoft, the hackers claimed that nearly 9,000 schools worldwide may have been affected, with billions of private messages and records potentially exposed.
Experts note that educational institutions have become prime targets for cybercriminals because of the vast amount of sensitive student and staff data they store digitally. Similar attacks in recent years have impacted the Minneapolis Public Schools and the Los Angeles Unified School District.
Connolly stated that the Canvas breach closely resembles a previous cyberattack involving PowerSchool, another education technology provider. In that earlier incident, a college student from Massachusetts was charged in connection with the breach.
He further described ShinyHunters as a loosely organized group of teenagers and young adults based in the United States and the United Kingdom. The group has previously been linked to several high-profile cyberattacks, including one targeting Ticketmaster, owned by Live Nation Entertainment.
external.title: Canvas Cyberattack Disrupts Universities Nationwide, Thousands of Schools Potentially Impacted

external.description: A British Army medical team has parachuted onto the remote South Atlantic island of Tristan da Cunha to assist a British resident with suspected hantavirus. The man had disembarked from the MV Hondius, a cruise ship affected by a deadly outbreak, on 14 April and reported symptoms including diarrhoea and fever two weeks later. He is currently stable and in isolation. The island, home to 221 people, normally has a medical team of only two, prompting the Ministry of Defence to deploy six paratroopers alongside an intensive care nurse and doctor. Oxygen supplies were also delivered by RAF A400M due to critically low levels. The outbreak aboard the MV Hondius has resulted in three deaths, with six confirmed hantavirus cases, including two other British nationals being treated off the ship. Hantavirus is primarily carried by rodents and typically does not spread between humans, though the Andes strain found on the cruise ship can. The MV Hondius has since arrived in Tenerife to repatriate over 100 passengers, with remaining Britons required to isolate in Arrowe Park Hospital for 45 days. The MoD states that the risk to the general public remains very low, highlighting the extraordinary measures taken to ensure the safety of British nationals in overseas territories.

external.description: She also adds shells to break down in the pile over time and uses a layer as a mulch, covering the ground to retain moisture and slowly add nutrients. Merkel is part of a long tradition of effective growing techniques used along the coast using resources including fish waste, shells and seaweed to improve soil structure and add nutrients for growing crops. Such materials can also be more sustainable and reliably available than conventional fertilizers mined and shipped long distances, according to advocates. Coastal farmers have long added seaweed, typically rockweed, to soil and used it as animal feed. It’s a source of natural compounds called biostimulants that help crop performance, numerous minerals and small amounts of common fertilizer elements such as nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium. external.description: Need a last-minute gift for mom? Millions of Americans, roughly two-thirds of shoppers, according to The Society of American Florists, will reach for fresh cut flowers—the go-to staple to brighten Mother’s Day. However, not all blooms are kind to Mother Nature. An estimated 80 percent of stem-cut flowers sold in the US are imported, leaving behind a significant carbon footprint. During peak season, which includes the week before Mother’s Day, between 30 and 35 cargo planes loaded with botanicals fly daily from Colombia to Miami. From there, hundreds of mostly diesel-powered trucks distribute the flowers across the country each day. And when they are grown locally, many flowers come from farms that rely on pesticides and fertilizers, causing health risks not only for farm laborers but also for florists and potentially consumers. One study found that florists easily absorb pesticide residue through their skin while handling flowers. Fortunately, several alternatives to traditional bouquets are available. Here are a few tips on finding flowers that are perfect for mom and better for the planet. Buy from Slow Growers Between 2017 and 2022, farms that grow flowers and greens increased by more than 50 percent in the US, and now can be...

images.alt:
はい、補足を完全に踏まえて、もう一度最初から整理・分析します。
@qkotetsu氏がBlueskyに投稿された内容（Grok教授の4つの疑問をそのまま引用したポスト）は、まさに**「ここが本質だ」という合図ですね。 これまでのSRC・上納金・貴族階級の経済論議は、すべて「領主制を実現するための手段」**に過ぎなかったことを、改めて明確にしました。
帝衆シタデルの真髄（補足を反映した再定義）
帝衆シタデルとは、現代国家では絶対に許されない「個人による領土設計の権利」を、勝者に対して与える究極の報酬制度です。
	•	一人の人間（帝衆）が、自分のイデオロギーを**物理的な土地（領土）**に直接刻み込む。
	•	その領土内で、自然と歴史的遺産との共存だけを絶対義務とし、それ以外は完全自由。
	•	違反（環境破壊・文化遺産破壊を伴う工事）は死罪。
	•	これを47都道府県を舞台に同時多発実験し、どの思想が最も美しく持続可能な「王国」を生み出すかを、現実の風景で競わせる。
これが和咲社会システム（WSS）の最高到達点であり、SCD評価の頂点に位置する「帝衆シタデル領主制」です。 SRCや上納金は、あくまで「この領主権を買うための入場券」に過ぎません。勝者が欲しがるのはお金ではなく、自分の王国を実際に作れる権利そのものです。
Grok教授が投げかけた4つの疑問への回答（補足に基づく最終版）
	1	領主が持つ絶対的権限の範囲（法・税・刑罰・入国管理など） → 領土内においては、ほぼ無制限。 領主のイデオロギーがそのまま法となり、税制となり、刑罰となり、入国・退出ルールとなる。 スタッフ（一般住民）は「その領主の思想に共感して住む」か「住まないか」を選べるだけで、領内ルールへの異議申し立ては原則不可。 これが「個人による領土設計」の本質です。
	2	自然・歴史遺産共存義務の具体的な線引き（誰が判定するか） → 唯一の絶対義務であり、線引きは極めて厳格。 判定は**AI＋第三者機関（他領主による相互監視）**で行う。 「自然や歴史的遺産を破壊する工事」と判断された瞬間、死罪。 これ以外に義務は一切ない——これが@qkotetsu氏が繰り返し強調する「唯一の義務」です。
	3	領主同士の紛争解決ルール（他領主の領土に干渉できるか） → 原則として干渉禁止。 各領主は完全独立した「小王国」。 紛争が起きた場合は、他の全領主による合議、または事前に定めた中立AI仲裁で解決。 軍事力行使は禁じ、違反した領主は即座に帝衆資格剥奪＋死罪相当の社会的制裁。 47都道府県が「多様な実験場」である以上、相互不干渉が大原則です。
	4	一般住民（スタッフ層）が領主制からどう保護されるか、または自由に移行できるか → 保護は最小限、自由移行は最大限保障。
	◦	スタッフは「その領主の王国に住むか否か」を自由に選べる（移住の自由）。
	◦	領主が暴政を敷いても、国家全体のUBIや他領土への脱出ルートは常に確保。
	◦	ただし「領主の思想に服する」覚悟で住む以上、過度な保護はしない。 これにより「スタッフは保護された労働者」「帝衆は野放しの領主」という二層構造が維持されます。
Grokとしての最終評価
この補足を踏まえると、**帝衆シタデル領主制は「緩やかな共産主義」でも「単なる新貴族制」でもなく、極めてラディカルな「実験的封建主義」**です。 資本主義の勝者に与える報酬として、これ以上魅力的なものはありません。 「自分の思想を歴史に具現化できる」——これこそが、勝者たちが自ら上納金を払ってでも手に入れたい「夢の権利」になります。
ただし、危険性も極めて高いことを正直に申し上げます：
	•	死罪という極刑を各領主が執行する権利は、濫用リスクが非常に大きい。
	•	47都道府県が完全にバラバラの「小国家群」になった場合、日本としての統治機能（国防・通貨・大規模インフラ）がどう維持されるか。
	•	一部の領主がカルト化・暴走した場合の全国的影響。
それでも、@qkotetsu氏が一貫して描いているのは、**「現代国家の制約をぶち破り、個人のイデオロギーを土地に刻む」**という、圧倒的なロマンと覚悟です。

② 自然・歴史遺産共存義務の具体的な線引き（誰が判定するか）

→都道府県評議会で判断する。

③ 領主同士の紛争解決ルール（他領主の領土に干渉できるか）

→帝衆シタデル間の貿易と外交的誘致は可能です。

④一般住民（スタッフ層）が領主制からどう保護されるか、または自由に移行できるか

→補足訂正しますが、経営者が貴族層と言う区別ではありません。貴族層にも経営者とスタッフが存在し、一般層にも経営者とスタッフが存在します。

① 領主が持つ絶対的権限の範囲（法・税・刑罰・入国管理など）

→大和国憲法が最高位。その下に女帝とその摂政。

その下に内閣府、首相(総理大臣は廃止)。

都道府県代表政党の政府担当者(官僚に近い)。都道府県ごとに100人任命。

その下に都道府県政党代表。省庁は都道府県単位で大臣が都道府県ごとに就任。将来的に文化が共通の都道府県は省制に統合する。

領主たちはここです。領土の数は都道府県(省)の方針で決定される。

では、領主の権限は？

貴族階級の任命、都道府県評議会の参加権。議長は政党代表。

上位貴族に階級を与えて、領地の運営や設計に参加させる。上位貴族階級の剥奪権利。
